assertions.text: V1 was traveling eastbound on the roadway.
The roadway was a two-lane, non-divided, bidirectional roadway.
The roadway had a slight curve to the left along the segment where V1 was traveling.
The roadway had a positive grade (uphill) along the segment where V1 was traveling.
V1 departed the roadway to the right.
V1's frontal plane impacted a large tree (impact to the front of V1).
After impact, V1 came to final rest in the eastbound lane of travel.
When V1 came to rest it was facing south.
There was one impact between V1 and the large tree.

verdicts.evidence: V1 was traveling eastbound is supported by the recorded speed showing forward motion before impact: [first_value 98.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s](T2) and [last_value 92.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2).
Roadway geometry (two-lane, non-divided, bidirectional) is not recorded by the vehicle sensors; no telemetry provides lane count or division, so this is unverifiable.
Vehicle telemetry does not record roadway curvature; no sensor channel documents road curve direction, so the claim of a slight left curve is unverifiable.
Vehicle sensors here do not report road grade; recorded speed and delta-V do not indicate uphill/downhill grade, so positive grade is unverifiable.

Departure direction (to the right) relative to roadway requires mapping between device axes and road orientation or GPS track; available telemetry lacks GPS position or heading, so departure to the right is unverifiable.

A frontal impact is supported by a large longitudinal crash pulse: [max_delta_v_long_kmh -95.0 at t=125.0 ms](T1) and [delta_v_long peak -95.0 at t=0.12 s](T2) with much smaller lateral delta-V: [max_delta_v_lat_kmh -7.0 at t=42.5 ms](T1), indicating the dominant impulse was in the vehicle long axis consistent with a frontal impact.
Final rest location (in the eastbound lane) is a positional claim; no GPS position or post-crash vehicle location is available in the telemetry, so this is unverifiable.
Vehicle-facing direction at rest (facing south) requires heading/attitude at final rest; available telemetry does not include absolute heading or final orientation, so this is unverifiable.

The EDR records a single crash event: [num_events_recorded 1](T1), and the crash-pulse data show a single dominant longitudinal delta-V spike reaching [95.0 km/h at t=0.12 s](T3), consistent with one impact.

result.rationale: EDR/telemetry strongly supports a primary frontal impact with a large longitudinal delta-V ([95.0 km/h peak](T3)) and contains speed history ([98.0 to 92.0 km/h pre-crash](T2)), but it lacks positional, heading, and roadway geometry sensors, making many narrative details unverifiable.

uncited_quantitative_claims.context: nant longitudinal delta-V spike reaching  , consistent with one impact.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
